entries.name: Braced
entries: When making an attack with a Braced weapon, use your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls. If the weapon has the {@itemProperty H|XPHB|Heavy} property, use your Strength score to determine if you can use it without {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.

entries.name: Burst
entries: As an action, you can expend 10 shots of a Burst weapon's ammunition to spray shots in a 10-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} within the weapon's normal range. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take damage. Roll the weapon's damage once, and apply it to each creature that failed the save.

entries.name: Close Quarters
entries: Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your ranged attack rolls with a Close Quarters weapon.

entries.name: Powered
entries: You can activate a Powered weapon that has a charged {@item Power Cell|NeonOdyssey} when you make an attack with it. On a hit when activated, the weapon's damage die changes to the value noted with the property. If a damage type is also noted, the weapon deals damage of that type when activated. If the weapon has the {@itemProperty V|XPHB|Versatile} property, the Powered property has a second notation for the activated damage when wielded with two hands.
A fully charged {@item Power Cell|NeonOdyssey} operates the weapon for 25 activated attacks, after which you must replace the cell as an action or a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

entries.name: Reload
entries: A Reload weapon's ammunition is held in a magazine or a {@item Power Cell|NeonOdyssey}. After the number of shots noted in the property, you must reload the weapon as an action or a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

entries.name: Scatter
entries: As an action, you can expend 2 shots from the weapon to fire it in a {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} within the specified area. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take damage. Roll the weapon's damage once, and apply it to each creature that failed the save.

item.name: Magazine
entries: A {@item Magazine|NeonOdyssey} holds up to 20 {@item Bullet|NeonOdyssey|Bullets}.

item.name: Case
entries: A {@item Case|NeonOdyssey} holds up to 20 {@item Shell|NeonOdyssey|Shells}.

item.name: Amplifier
entries: An Amplifier is a speaker that amplifies sounds so they can be heard up to 300 feet away. It comes with a hand-held microphone, and can be paired with a {@item Communicator|NeonOdyssey}, a data storage device for playing audio files, or a {@item Musician's Instrument|NeonOdyssey}.

item.name: Ammunition
entries: Ammunition is required by a weapon that has the {@itemProperty A|XPHB|Ammunition} property. A weapon's description specifies the type of ammunition used by the weapon. The Ammunition table lists the different types and the amount you get when you buy them. The table also lists the item that is typically used to store each type; storage must be bought separately.

item.name: Ascension Gun
entries: An Ascension Gun is a compact device with a high-strength cable and anchoring head fired by gas or magnetic acceleration. As a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, you can fire the gun at a surface or object you can see within 100 feet of yourself, and the head anchors if you succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}) check. As part of the same action, or as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on subsequent turns, you can retract the cable 25 feet, hauling up to 500 pounds toward the anchor point, or a weight lighter than you and no more than 500 pounds toward yourself.

item.name: Camera
entries: A Camera can take still pictures or record video and audio. It stores recorded media on a removable {@item Data Chip|NeonOdyssey}.

item.name: Canteen
entries: A Canteen holds up to a half gallon. If you don't drink sufficient water, you risk {@hazard Dehydration|XPHB}.

item.name: Case, Shell
entries: A Shell Case holds up to 10 {@item Shell|XPHB|Shells}.

item.name: Climbing Kit
entries: A Climbing Kit includes boot tips, gloves, pitons, and a harness. As a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, you can use the Climber's Kit to anchor yourself; when you do, you can't fall more than 25 feet from the anchor point, and you can't move more than 25 feet from there without undoing the anchor as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

item.name: Clothing, Designer
entries: Designer Clothes are made of expensive fabrics and adorned with expertly crafted details. Some events and locations admit only people wearing these clothes.

item.name: Clothing, Traveling
entries: Traveling Clothes are resilient garments designed for travel in various environments.

item.name: Communicator, High Power
entries: A High Power Communicator includes a backpack power and central unit with a compact antenna, and a headset or hand transceiver. It can also be easily mounted in a vehicle. A High Power Communicator can connect to a communication network, or it can directly connect to another comm device in a range up to 25 miles with direct line of sight. It can store comm codes or select frequencies. If you use the comm in an area of jamming, if you succeed on a DC 15 Intelligence ({@skill Computers|NeonOdyssey}) check, the comm functions until the start of your next turn. A {@item Power Cell|NeonOdyssey} powers the unit for 24 hours of use.
If you aren't maintaining a Lifestyle, the communicator can't connect to a comm network.

item.name: Communicator, Personal
entries: A Personal Communicator is a Tiny device easily held in the palm of one hand. It can connect to a communication network to allow long distance calling or directly connect to another comm device over a short range—1 mile with direct line of sight, or 1,000 feet in a city or similar terrain. It has its own unique comm code and can store hundreds of codes for easy calling. The communicator functions for 48 hours before needing to be recharged (1 hour connected to a power source).
If you aren't maintaining a Lifestyle, the communicator can't connect to a comm network.

item.name: Cord
entries: Cord is 10 feet long. You can tie a knot in it as a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action.

item.name: Cosmic Symbol
entries: A Cosmic Symbol takes one of the forms in the Cosmic Symbol table and is machined or inscribed to channel cosmic magic. An Enforcer or Oracle can use a Cosmic Symbol as a Spellcasting Focus.
The table indicates whether a Cosmic Symbol needs to be held, worn, or born on fabric (such as clothing or a banner) or a suit of armor or a Shield.

entries.caption: Cosmic Symbols
rows: Insignia (on fabric, armor, or a Shield)

item.name: Data Chip
entries: A Data Chip is a digital storage unit easily held in the palm of a hand. You can insert the chip into {@item Datapad|NeonOdyssey|Datapads}, computers, or other devices to access, transfer, and store information. A standard Data Chip can store 4,000 pages of simple, text-only data—about 100 books worth. The same device can hold 400 photos or other still images, 200 hours of audio, 48 hours of high-definition video, or 3 high complexity computer programs.

item.name: Datapad
entries: A Datapad is a device that stores data in the form of text, audio, or video files, has simple audio-video recording capability, and can execute simple programs. If you consult an accurate nonfiction Datapad file about its topic, you gain a +5 bonus to Intelligence ({@skill Arcana|XPHB}, {@skill Computers|NeonOdyssey}, {@skill History|XPHB}, {@skill Nature|XPHB}, {@skill Religion|XPHB}, or {@skill Technology|NeonOdyssey}) checks you make about that topic.
The pad's information is stored on a removable {@item Data Chip|NeonOdyssey} (included with a newly purchased or crafted Datapad).

item.name: Disguise
entries: While wearing a Disguise, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any ability check you make to impersonate the person or type of person it represents.

item.name: Flamethrower
entries: A Flamethrower is a pistol-like device with a pressure nozzle and an igniter beneath it. When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can replace one of your attacks with firing the Flamethrower. Each creature in a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or take {@damage 2d4} Fire damage. Flammable objects in the {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} that aren't being worn or carried begin {@hazard Burning|XPHB}. After three uses, you must reload the Flamethrower with a Fuel Oil flask as an action or a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

item.name: Flashlight
entries: A Flashlight is a handheld device that uses a {@item Power Cell|NeonOdyssey} to produce light. You can turn the light on or off (no action required) once during your turn. A {@item Power Cell|NeonOdyssey} powers a Flashlight for 10 hours of use.
The Flashlight has three modes, which you can switch as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

entries.name: Ambient
entries: The light produces {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 30-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 30 feet.

entries.name: Spotlight
entries: The light produces {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 60-foot
{@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 60 feet.

entries.name: Subtle
entries: The light produces {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} in a 5-foot radius.

item.name: Grapnel
entries: As a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, you can throw the Grapnel at a railing, a ledge, or another catch within 50 feet of yourself, and the hook catches on if you succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}) check. If you tied a {@item Synth Line|NeonOdyssey} to the hook, you can then climb it.

item.name: Grav Spikes
entries: As a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, you can spread Grav Spikes to cover a 5-foot-square area within 5 feet of yourself. A creature that enters this area for the first time on a turn must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 1} Piercing damage and have its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn. It takes 10 minutes to recover the Grav Spikes.

item.name: Healing Stim
entries: A Healing Stim is a hypertech version of a {@item Potion of Healing|XDMG}. This injector, medicine elixir, or dermal patch is a magic item. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can administer it to yourself or another creature within 5 feet of you. The target regains {@dice 2d4 + 2} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

item.name: Holoprojector
entries: A Holoprojector is a compact, portable holo emitter with limited storage, about the size of a deck of cards. You can activate or deactivate it with a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, and you can select a new stored image as part of the same action. While activated, it projects a holographic image up to 5 feet away from it. The projector can store up to 10 images. An image can be an object, a creature, or a scene such as a photograph, and it can be animated with one round worth of movement or apparent actions, which can loop. The projector has enough power to operate for 10 minutes before needing to be recharged (which takes 1 hour connected to a power source), or it can be wired into a power source for continuous function.

Physical contact reveals the hologram for what it is. A creature can take the {@action Study|XPHB} action to examine the image, and determines its true nature with a successful DC 13 Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check.

item.name: Igniter
entries: An Igniter is a small device that uses sparks and simple fuel to create a small flame. Using it to light a candle or anything with exposed fuel or constructed of flammable material takes a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. Lighting any other fire takes 1 minute.

item.name: Intruder's Pack
entries: An Intruder's Pack contains the following items: {@item Rucksack|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Canteen|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Flashlight|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Grapnel|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Igniter|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Motion Sensor|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Power Cell|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Pry Bar|NeonOdyssey}, 5 {@item Ration Pouch|NeonOdyssey|Ration Pouches}, {@item Steel Bearings|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Synth Line|NeonOdyssey}.

item.name: Link Cable
entries: A 20-foot spool of cable with connectors at each end for transferring power or data.

item.name: Macro Scope
entries: A Macro Scope is an optical enhancer for viewing distant things. Standard, commercially available models allow detailed viewing out to double a user's normal sight range. More expensive variant models add different functionality, such as longer range or vision modes.

As a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, you can fit a scope to a ranged weapon with the {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed} property. Ranged attack rolls with the weapon don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} for firing at a target beyond close range if you don't move during the same turn.

item.name: Medic's Kit
entries: A Medic's Kit has ten uses. As a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, you can expend one of its uses to stabilize an {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} creature that has 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} without needing to make a Wisdom ({@skill Medicine|XPHB}) check.

item.name: Motion Sensor
entries: A Tiny device that detects movement within 5 feet of its front. You can activate or deactivate it as a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, and it has an adhesive pad on the back that allows it to be affixed to a surface as part of the same action. While activated, when a Small or larger creature or object moves within 5 feet of the sensor's front, it emits a sound that can be heard up to 60 feet away. It can operate for 24 hours before needing to be recharged (which takes 1 hour connected to a power source), or it can be wired into a power source for continuous function.

item.name: Night Vision Goggles
entries: These goggles enhance existing light and use infrared light sources to provide you with {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. The goggles require a {@item Power Cell|NeonOdyssey} (included) and function for 8 hours on a full cell.

item.name: Outrunner's Pack
entries: An Outrunner's Pack contains the following items: {@item Rucksack|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Canteen|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Flashlight|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Grav Spikes|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Igniter|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Power Cell|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Pry Bar|NeonOdyssey}, 10 {@item Ration Pouch|NeonOdyssey|Ration Pouches}, {@item Synth Line|NeonOdyssey}.

item.name: Performer's Pack
entries: A Performer's Pack contains the following items: {@item Rucksack|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Amplifier|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Camera|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Canteen|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Data Chip|NeonOdyssey}, 3 {@item Disguise|NeonOdyssey|Disguises}, {@item Flashlight|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Igniter|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Motion Sensor|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Power Cell|NeonOdyssey}, 10 {@item Ration Pouch|NeonOdyssey|Ration Pouches}, {@item Sleeping Bag|NeonOdyssey}.

item.name: Power Cell
entries: Power Cells are compact energy storage devices compatible with a wide spectrum of equipment. A standard Power Cell can't be recharged.

entries.name: Energy Weapons
entries: A Power Cell powers a ranged weapon that uses it for ammunition or a powered melee weapon. The weapons' properties list how many shots or attacks a cell can power.

entries.name: Powered Devices
entries: The description of a piece of equipment that requires a Power Cell for power notes how long the cell can power it. Changing a Power Cell in a device requires either an action or {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

item.name: Primal Focus
entries: A Primal Focus takes one of the forms in the Primal Focuses table and is carved, chiseled, or painted to channel primal magic. A {@class Primalist|NeonOdyssey} or {@class Tracer|NeonOdyssey} can use such an object as a {@item Spellcasting Focus|XPHB}.

item.name: Pry Bar
entries: Using a Pry Bar gives you {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength checks where the Pry Bar's leverage can be applied.

item.name: Ration Pouch
entries: Ration Packs consist of travel-ready food, including dried meat and fruit, dense bread, nuts, and shelf stable pouches or hard containers.

item.name: Rucksack
entries: A Rucksack holds up to 30 pounds within 1 cubic foot. It can also serve as a saddlebag for a mount, or a motorcycle or similar vehicle.

item.name: Silencer
entries: A Silencer is a device that dissipates escaping propellant gases when fitted to the muzzle of a Ranged weapon that fires {@item Bullet|NeonOdyssey|Bullets} and has a damage die of {@dice 1d8} or smaller. You can attach or remove a Silencer with a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action. A listener within 30 feet of a silenced gunshot hears the muffled shot with a successful DC 20 Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) check.
A weapon with a Silencer attached has its range halved.

item.name: Sleeping Bag
entries: A Sleeping Bag sleeps one Small or Medium creature. While in a Sleeping Bag, you automatically succeed on saving throws against {@hazard Extreme Cold|XPHB}.

item.name: Steel Bearings
entries: As a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, you can spill Steel Bearings from their pouch. They spread to cover a level, 10-foot-square area within 10 feet of yourself. A creature that enters this area for the first time on a turn must succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity saving throw or have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. It takes 10 minutes to recover the Steel Bearings.

item.name: Synth Line
entries: A 50-foot length of synthetic fiber rope. As a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, you can tie a knot with Synth Line if you succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB}) check. The Synth Line can be burst with a successful DC 20 Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check.

You can bind an unwilling creature with the Synth Line only if the creature has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, or {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition. If the creature's legs are bound, the creature has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until it escapes. Escaping the Synth Line requires the creature to make a successful DC 15 Dexterity ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check as an action.

item.name: Technician's Pack
entries: A Technician's Pack contains the following items: {@item Rucksack|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Cord|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Datapad|NeonOdyssey} (technical schematic), {@item Flashlight|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Igniter|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Link Cable|NeonOdyssey}, 2 {@item Power Cell|NeonOdyssey|Power Cells}, {@item Pry Bar|NeonOdyssey}, 5 {@item Ration Pouch|NeonOdyssey|Ration Pouches}, {@item Synth Line|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Thermal Blanket|NeonOdyssey}.

item.name: Thermal Blanket
entries: A waterproof, reflective blanket also useful as a drop cloth or tarp. While wrapped in a Thermal Blanket, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against {@hazard Extreme Cold|XPHB}.

item.name: Trench Coat
entries: A Trench Coat is a practical or vocational garment. It is waterproof, has many pockets, and can conceal larger carried objects.

{@i Choose A or B:} (A) {@item Light Armor|NeonOdyssey} ({@item Composite Weave|NeonOdyssey}), {@item Quickblade|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Laser Rifle|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Shotgun|NeonOdyssey}, 20 {@item Shell|NeonOdyssey|Shells}, 2 {@item Power Cell|NeonOdyssey|Power Cells}, {@item Primal Focus|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Outrunner's Pack|NeonOdyssey}, and 870 GN; or (B) 10,000 GN

classFeature.name: Hunter's Eye
entries: You always have the {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it twice without expending a spell slot, and you regain all expended uses of this ability when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
The number of times you can cast the spell without a spell slot increases when you reach certain Tracer levels, as shown in the Hunter's Eye column of the Tracer Features table.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: Through your instinctive connection to the primordial archetype of the hunter, you have learned to cast spells. See the D&D Beyond Basic Rules for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Tracer spells, which appear in the Tracer spell list later in the class's description.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Tracer Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Prepared Spells of Level 1+
entries: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two level 1 Tracer spells. {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB} and {@spell Ensnaring Strike|XPHB} are recommended.

The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Tracer levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Tracer Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Tracer spells until the number of spells on your list matches the number in the Tracer Features table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you're a level 5 Tracer, your list of prepared spells can include six Tracer spells of level 1 or 2 in any combination.

If another Tracer feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Tracer spells for you.

entries.name: Changing Your Prepared Spells
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace one spell on your list with another Tracer spell for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your Tracer spells.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use a {@item Primal Focus|NeonOdyssey} or a Simple or Martial weapon as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Tracer spells.

classFeature.name: Weapon Mastery
entries: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency, such as {@item Laser Rifle|NeonOdyssey|Laser Rifles} and {@item Quickblade|NeonOdyssey|Quickblades}.

Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose. For example, you could switch to using the mastery properties of {@item Saber|NeonOdyssey|Sabers} and {@item Assault Rifle|NeonOdyssey|Assault Rifles}.

classFeature.name: Adaptive Edge
entries: Your primal affinity for detail and adaptation grants you the following benefits.

entries.name: Expertise
entries: Choose one of your skill proficiencies with which you lack {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB}. You gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in that skill.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You know two languages of your choice.

classFeature.name: Fighting Style
entries: You gain a {@filter Fighting Style feat|feats|category=FS} of your choice. Instead of choosing one of those feats, you can choose the option below.

classFeature.name: Tracer Subclass
entries: You gain a Tracer subclass of your choice. The Powertech subclass is detailed after this class's description. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Tracer levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Tracer level or lower.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Ranger levels 8, 12, and 16.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.

classFeature.name: Omni-Traversal
entries: Terrain no longer bars the path to your quarry. Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing Heavy armor. You also have a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} and a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Tracer Subclass.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Expertise
entries: Choose two of your skill proficiencies with which you lack {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB}. You gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in those skills.

classFeature.name: Outlast
entries: You can draw upon your primordial connection to the hunt to shake off injury and fatigue to keep going. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Temporary Hit Points
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can give yourself a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB|Temporary Hit Point} equal to {@dice 1d8} plus your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1). You can use this action a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Decrease Exhaustion
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, your {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level, if any, decreases by 1.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Tracer Subclass.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Tenacious Trace
entries: Your focus is adamant when locked on your quarry. Taking damage can't break your {@status Concentration|XPHB} on {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}.

classFeature.name: Hunter's Ferocity
entries: When you mark your prey, there is no escape. When you cast {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}, you can modify the spell so it doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}. If you do, the spell's duration becomes 1 minute for that casting, and it ends early if you cast {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB} again.

classFeature.name: Cloaking
entries: As a cosmic hunter, you can magically hide yourself in your surroundings. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can give yourself the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Tracer Subclass.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Lock-On
entries: Your chosen quarry stands out sharply to your senses. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against the creature currently marked by your {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}.

classFeature.name: Instinctive Scan
entries: You constantly scan your surroundings and process the tiniest details. This grants you {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet.

classFeature.name: Epic Boon
entries: You gain an {@filter Epic Boon feat|feats|category=EB} or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Dimensional Travel|XPHB} is recommended.

classFeature.name: Inescapable Hunt
entries: Your primal connection pushes your hunter's instinct to its apex, granting the following benefits.

entries.name: Amplified Instincts
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can cast {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB} on it as part of the attack and can ignore the spell's normal range. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Apex Predator
entries: When you use your Hunter's Ferocity feature, instead of making the spell's duration 1 minute, you can make it permanent. The spell now ends immediately if the target dies or if you cast {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB} again.

subclassFeature.name: Powertech
entries: {@i Bond with Armor to Become the Ultimate Hunter}

Powertechs channel their Tracer magic through technology to create enhanced suits of armor. Into this armor, they incorporate swappable weapons and gear to become implacable hunters who can adapt to evolving prey.

subclassFeature.name: Powertech Spells
entries: When you reach a Tracer level specified in the Powertech Spells table, thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Powertech Tools
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Armor Training
entries: You gain training with Heavy armor.

entries.name: Tool Proficiency
entries: You gain a set of {@item Mechanic's Tools|NeonOdyssey}, and you have proficiency with them.

subclassFeature.name: Hunter's Armor
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while you have {@item Mechanic's Tools|NeonOdyssey} in hand, you can turn a suit of armor you are wearing into Hunter's Armor. The armor continues to be Hunter's Armor until you don another suit of armor or if you die.
You gain the following benefits while wearing your Hunter's Armor.

entries.name: Fast Armoring
entries: You can don or doff the armor as a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action. The armor can't be removed against your will.

entries.name: Incorporate Gear
entries: You create and install one of the following pieces of adventuring gear into your armor (ignoring its weight), and you can take the {@action Utilize|XPHB} action with it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}: {@item Ascension Gun|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Bypass Tools|NeonOdyssey} (you have proficiency with it), {@item Climbing Kit|NeonOdyssey}, {@item Macro Scope|NeonOdyssey} (affects your Pulse Cannon, see below, regardless of its properties), or {@item Medic's Kit|NeonOdyssey} (replenishes {@dice 1d6} uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}).

The gear can't be removed from the armor, but you can change the selection when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. The gear vanishes if the armor stops being your Hunter's Armor.

entries.name: Second Skin
entries: If the armor normally has a Strength requirement, the Hunter's Armor lacks this requirement for you. Additionally, it doesn't count as Heavy armor for your {@classFeature Omni-Traversal|Tracer|NeonOdyssey|6} feature.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use the Hunter's Armor as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Tracer spells. When you do so, a spell lacks a Verbal component and gains an Material component.

subclassFeature.name: Pulse Cannon
entries: Your armor contains a pulse cannon weapon system, granting the following benefits.

entries.name: Arm Cannon
entries: While wearing your {@subclassFeature Hunter's Armor|Tracer|NeonOdyssey|Powertech|NeonOdyssey|3}, you can activate or deactivate the pulse cannon whenever you can draw or stow a weapon. While activated, the gauntlet and forearm of one of the armor's arms transform into the cannon. Your hand inside the cannon can't hold or manipulate other objects.

While the cannon is active, you can fire it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. The cannon is a Martial Ranged weapon with a normal range of 100 feet and a long range of 300 feet. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d8} Force damage, and you can activate the {@itemMastery Push|XPHB} or {@itemMastery Sap|XPHB} mastery property. You can use this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Integrate Weapon
entries: Using {@item Mechanic's Tools|NeonOdyssey}, when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can integrate (or remove) a Ranged weapon into the armor, causing the weapon to vanish. The weapon reappears in the armor's space when it is removed or when the armor is no longer your {@subclassFeature Hunter's Armor|Tracer|NeonOdyssey|Powertech|NeonOdyssey|3}.
You can have one weapon integrated into the armor at a time, and you can't drop the integrated weapon. While the cannon is active, you can fire the integrated weapon normally.

subclassFeature.name: Mark II Armor
entries: You make substantive upgrades to your {@subclassFeature Hunter's Armor|Tracer|NeonOdyssey|Powertech|NeonOdyssey|3} designs, gaining the following benefits.

entries.name: Gear Expansion
entries: You create and install one of the following pieces of adventuring gear into your armor (ignoring its weight): {@item Flamethrower|NeonOdyssey} (refuels when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}), {@item Communicator, High Power|NeonOdyssey|High Power Communicator} (provides its own power), or {@item Night Vision Goggles|NeonOdyssey} (provides its own power).

The gear can't be removed from the armor, but you can change the selection when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. The gear vanishes if the armor stops being your {@subclassFeature Hunter's Armor|Tracer|NeonOdyssey|Powertech|NeonOdyssey|3}.

entries.name: Flight Jets
entries: You build flight systems into your armor. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while wearing your {@subclassFeature Hunter's Armor|Tracer|NeonOdyssey|Powertech|NeonOdyssey|3}, you gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} for 1 minute or until you remove the armor.
You can use this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Power Cycler
entries: You add power reserves to your {@subclassFeature Hunter's Armor|Tracer|NeonOdyssey|Powertech|NeonOdyssey|3} that activate when needed, granting you the following benefits while wearing the armor.

entries.name: Capacitor Spike
entries: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, if you have fewer than two uses remaining for the Arm Cannon benefit of your {@subclassFeature Pulse Cannon|Tracer|NeonOdyssey|Powertech|NeonOdyssey|3} feature, you regain uses until you have two.

entries.name: Stim Jolt
entries: When you fail a saving throw, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Mark III Armor
entries: You make a final upgrade to your {@subclassFeature Hunter's Armor|Tracer|NeonOdyssey|Powertech|NeonOdyssey|3} and gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Overcharged Pulse
entries: On a hit, your {@subclassFeature Pulse Cannon|Tracer|NeonOdyssey|Powertech|NeonOdyssey|3} now deals {@damage 2d8} Force damage, and you can activate the {@itemMastery Topple|XPHB} mastery property.

entries.name: Reinforce Plating
entries: Your {@subclassFeature Hunter's Armor|Tracer|NeonOdyssey|Powertech|NeonOdyssey|3} provides the Material component when you cast {@spell Stoneskin|XPHB}. You can also cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in this way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

Whenever you start casting the spell while wearing your {@subclassFeature Hunter's Armor|Tracer|NeonOdyssey|Powertech|NeonOdyssey|3}, you can modify it so that it doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}. If you do so, the spell's duration becomes 1 minute for that casting, and you gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Radiant damage for the duration.

feat.name: Primal Hunter

otherSummary.entry: When Gaining the Level 2 Tracer "Fighting Style" Feature

entries: You learn two {@filter Primalist cantrips|spells|level=0|class=druid} of your choice (see the Primalist class's section for a list of Primalist spells). {@spell Guidance|XPHB} and {@spell Starry Wisp|XPHB} are recommended. The chosen cantrips count as Tracer spells for you, and Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for them. Whenever you gain a Tracer level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another Primalist cantrip.

spell.name: Summon Discordant

components.m: a broken tuning fork worth 6,000+ GN

entries: You call forth a discordant fiendish spirit from Cacophony. It manifests in an unoccupied space that you can see within range and uses the {@creature Discordant Spirit|NeonOdyssey} stat block. When you cast the spell, choose the creature's tone: Entropy, Instinct, Progress, or Thrill. The spirit resembles a Fiend of the chosen tone, which determines certain details of its stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or when the spell ends.

The creature is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, the creature shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and uses its movement to avoid danger.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: Use the spell slot's level for the spell's level in the stat block.

hp.special: 50 (Entropy or Instinct) or 40 (Progress) or 60 (Thrill) + 15 for each spell level above 6

trait.name: Blood Frenzy (Instinct Only)
entries: The spirit has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against a creature that doesn't have all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The spirit has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Pinpoint Targeting (Progress Only)
entries: The spirit's attack rolls ignore {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} and {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three-Quarters Cover}, and an enemy withing 5 feet of the spirit doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on its ranged attack rolls.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The spirit makes a number of attacks equal to half this spell's level (round down).

action.name: Corrosive Claw (Instinct Only)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, reach 5 ft. {@h}{@damage 2d6 + 3 + summonSpellLevel} Acid damage.

action.name: Drain Life (Entropy Only)
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft. {@h}{@damage 2d6 + 3 + summonSpellLevel} Necrotic damage, and the target can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} until the start of the spirit's next turn.

action.name: Juggling Knife (Thrill Only)
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., {@h}{@damage 2d6 + 3 + summonSpellLevel} Psychic damage, and the target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the start of the spirit's next turn.

action.name: Plasma Rifle (Progress Only)
entries: {@atkr r} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, range 150 ft., {@h}{@damage 2d6 + 3 + summonSpellLevel} Lightning damage.

trait.name: Undead Fortitude
entries: If damage reduces the drift gaunt to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it makes a Constitution saving throw ({@dc 5} plus the damage taken) unless the damage is Fire or from a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}. On a successful save, the drift gaunt drops to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The drift gaunt makes two Irradiated Slam attacks or two Blaster attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Unmask if available.

action.name: Irradiated Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Necrotic damage.

action.name: Laser Carbine
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 5}, range 80/240 ft. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) Radiant damage.

action.name: Unmask (1/Day)
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 13}, each nonUndead creature in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the drift gaunt. {@actSaveFail} 11 ({@damage 2d10}) Necrotic damage, and the creature has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the start of the drift gaunt's next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

bonus.name: Air Jets
entries: The drift gaunt releases compressed air to move up to 10 feet in any direction without provoking Opportunity Attacks. This distance is doubled in environments with reduced or no gravity.

trait.name: Jetpack
entries: The drift gaunt wears a damaged jetpack on its back.
While wearing it, the drift gaunt has a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 40 ft., can hover, and can use the Rocket Jets {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

The jetpack has AC 13; HP 25; and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Necrotic, Poison, and Psychic damage. If the jetpack is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it explodes. {@actSave dex} {@dc 14}, each creature in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the jetpack. The drift gaunt automatically fails this save. {@actSaveFail} 21 ({@damage 6d6}) Fire damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

trait.name: Undead Fortitude
entries: If damage reduces the drift gaunt to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it makes a Constitution saving throw ({@dc 5} plus the damage taken) unless the damage is Fire or from a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}. On a successful save, the drift gaunt drops to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The drift gaunt makes three Irradiated Slam attacks or three Plasma Rifle attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Unmask if available.

action.name: Irradiated Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 8}, reach 5 ft. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Necrotic damage.

action.name: Plasma Rifle
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 8}, range 120/400 ft. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) Lightning damage. Until the start of the drift gaunt's next turn, the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved, and the next attack against the target has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

action.name: Unmask (1/Day)
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 14}, each nonUndead creature in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the drift gaunt. {@actSaveFail} 16 ({@damage 3d10}) Necrotic damage, and the target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the start of the drift gaunt's next turn.

bonus.name: Rocket Jets (Requires Jetpack)
entries: The drift gaunt fires its jetpack burners to move up to 40 feet in any direction without provoking Opportunity Attacks. This distance is doubled in environments with reduced or no gravity.

trait.name: Personal Warp Generator
entries: The drift gaunt wears a damaged personal warp generator on its back. While wearing it, the drift gaunt has a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 40 ft., can hover, and can use the Warp Cells {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

The generator has AC 15; HP 40; and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Necrotic, Poison, and Psychic damage. If the generator is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it explodes. {@actSave str} {@dc 16}, each creature in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the generator. The drift gaunt automatically fails this save. {@actSaveFail} 35 ({@damage 10d6}) Force damage, and the target is pulled 10 feet toward the generator. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

trait.name: Undead Fortitude
entries: If damage reduces the drift gaunt to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it makes a Constitution saving throw ({@dc 5} plus the damage taken) unless the damage is Fire or from a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}. On a successful save, the drift gaunt drops to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The drift gaunt makes three Irradiated Slam attacks or three Plasma Cannon attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Death's-Head Visage if available.

action.name: Irradiated Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, reach 5 ft. {@h}15 ({@damage 3d6 + 5}) Bludgeoning damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Necrotic damage. If the target is a creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 15}) from one of two hands.

action.name: Plasma Cannon
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 10}, range 120/400 ft. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d8 + 5}) Lightning damage. Until the start of the drift gaunt's next turn, the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved, and the next attack against the target has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

action.name: Death's-Head Visage (1/Day)
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 16}, each non-Undead creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 16 ({@damage 3d10}) Psychic damage, and the target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically. While {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, the target has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition.

bonus.name: Commanding Presence
entries: The drift gaunt chooses one of its allies within 30 feet. That creature can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make an attack against a target that it and the drift gaunt can see.

bonus.name: Warp Cells (Requires Personal Warp Generator)
entries: The drift gaunt activates its personal warp generator to teleport, along with one creature it is grappling, up to 120 feet in any direction.

trait.name: Spatial Distortion
entries: Attack rolls against the manta have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}, since it is slightly out of sync with the fabric of space. This trait is suppressed while the manta has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The manta makes two Tail attacks and uses Voidshadow if available.

action.name: Tail
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 8}, reach 10 ft. {@h}22 ({@damage 4d8 + 4}) Piercing damage. If the target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, the manta regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the damage taken. It can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} in this way more than once per turn.

action.name: Voidshadow {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 15}, each creature in a 60-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} The target takes 21 ({@damage 4d8 + 3}) Psychic damage and has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition. The target repeats the save at the end of its each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

bonus.name: Void Slip
entries: The manta teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. The destination space must be within 5 feet of another void manta or a creature with the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition. The manta gains {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the first attack it makes before the end of its turn.

trait.name: Adaptable Biology
entries: The dolphin can breathe in any environment, even one with no atmosphere, and is immune to aether exposure.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dolphin makes three Slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d10 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage plus 11 ({@damage 2d10}) Force damage.

action.name: Echolocation Burst {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 14}, each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Force damage. Until the end of the dolphin's next turn, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

action.name: Song of Time {@recharge}
entries: The dolphin and each creature of its choice in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the dolphin gains {@dice 2d10} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}. In addition, an affected target can reroll one {@dice D20} Test it makes before the start of the dolphin's next turn.

bonus.name: Tesseract
entries: The dolphin teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

reaction.name: Rewind
entries: {@actTrigger} The dolphin is hit with an attack roll or fails a saving throw. {@actResponse} The attacker must reroll the attack roll and use the new roll or the dolphin rerolls the saving throw and uses the new roll.

skill.name: Computers
entries: The Computers skill governs knowledge, use, and creation of software to run on computers or other electronic devices. Use Computers for tasks relating to manipulating software or operating systems. Some tasks involving the Computers skill require a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, but many are more involved and take more time to perform.

entries.name: Research
entries: Using computer databases or Hypergrid browsers for a simple, public information search doesn't require an ability check. Such searches usually only require a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, but more protected information takes longer to locate, as shown in the Computer Research table. At the end of that time, the character locates the desired Information with a successful Intelligence (Computers or {@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check using access to an appropriate database. The obscurity of the information sets the DC, as shown on the Computer Research table.

Information ranges from general to protected. General information is readily available and usually doesn't require an ability check. Specific information could be personal details such as a date of birth. Restricted information is more sensitive such as a home address or personal comm frequency. Protected information is the most difficult to locate, such as financial data or non-public legal records.

entries.name: Ship Systems
entries: Some starship or other vehicle systems operate using the crew's proficiency in the Computers skill. For example, scanning with a starship's sensors might require a Wisdom check using the ship's Wisdom score and the operator's Computers skill proficiency (see "Vehicles").

skill.name: Technology
entries: The Technology skill governs knowledge, use, and creation or repair of technological hardware and machines. This skill can also be used to identify and operate hypertech.

entries.name: Repairs
entries: Repairing damage to vehicles or ending mishaps on them or a Spiker's Deck uses a character's proficiency in the Technology skill.

entries.name: Technical Knowledge
entries: A character's proficiency in the Technology skill can be used to understand the purpose and functions of a machine or component of a larger physical system.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Small (about 3-4 feet tall)

entries.name: Dazzle
entries: You know the {@spell Dancing Lights|XPHB} cantrip.

Additionally, starting at character level 3, you always have {@spell Color Spray|XPHB} prepared, and starting at character level 5, you always have {@spell Calm Emotions|XPHB} prepared. You can cast each spell once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells (choose the ability when you select this species).

entries.name: Emotional Spectrum
entries: You have a supernatural affinity for heightening emotions through light. Choose one of the options below; when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can choose a new option. Your chosen option changes your skin, hair, or eye color. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, for the next minute, you shed {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet. You infuse the light with your chosen color to provide its benefit, and you can change your chosen color as part of the {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
You can use this trait a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

items.name: Blue or Teal
entries: You and your allies within the light have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Psychic damage and have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws made to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions.

items.name: Green
entries: When you or your allies in the light regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to the number regained.

items.name: Orange
entries: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet. When an ally starts its turn in the light or enters the light for the first time on a turn, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Pink or Purple
entries: Ability checks made to use the {@action Influence|XPHB} action on an {@variantrule Indifferent [Attitude]|XPHB|Indifferent} creature in the light have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

items.name: Yellow
entries: Double the range of your shed light. You and your allies in the light have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) and Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks. The first time an affected creature misses with an attack roll, it can add {@dice 1d4} to the total, possibly turning the miss into a hit.

entries.name: Passionate Presence
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Deception|XPHB}, {@skill Performance|XPHB}, or {@skill Persuasion|XPHB} skill.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 6-7 feet tall)

entries.name: Fury of the Tempest
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Athletics|XPHB}, {@skill Intimidation|XPHB}, or {@skill Survival|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Storm Magic
entries: You know the {@spell Shocking Grasp|XPHB} and {@spell Thunder Burst|NeonOdyssey} cantrips, and Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for them (choose when you select this species). You can cast the spells as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Tempest Resistance
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning damage and Thunder damage.

entries.name: Unleash the Storm
entries: When you reach character level 3, you can release the storm within your being as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, using one of the options below (choose the option each time you use this trait). The storm lasts for 1 minute or until you end it (no action required). Once you take this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

For the duration, you create a storm in 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you, and once on each of your turns, when you deal damage with an attack or a spell, you can deal extra damage to one target. The extra damage equals your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and the extra damage's type is Lightning for Lightning Spirit, Cold for Tempest Born, or Thunder for Thunder Soul.
Here are the storm options:

items.name: Lightning Spirit
entries: Lightning lances out from you. At the end of each of your turns, each creature of your choice in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} takes Lightning damage equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

items.name: Tempest Born
entries: Rain sheets lash around you. You and your allies in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} have {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} from ranged attacks.

items.name: Thunder Soul
entries: Thunder rolls in waves from you. At the start of each of your turns, each creature of your choice in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} and the ability modifier you chose for Storm Magic) or be pushed up to 10 feet away from you. A creature that fails the save by 5 or more has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

items.name: Wrathful Resilience
entries: If you fail a Strength or Constitution saving throw, you can expend a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} to reroll the {@dice d20} and use the new roll.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 5-6 feet tall)

entries.name: Enduring Passion
entries: When you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of you fails a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to give the target {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB}. The target can choose to use it on the triggering roll. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Illuminate
entries: You know the {@spell Light|XPHB} cantrip. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it (choose the ability when you select this species).

entries.name: Lightbringer's Glare
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can unleash a blaze of solar radiance. Each creature of your choice within a 15-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you takes Radiant damage equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. You can use this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Phoenix Rising
entries: Immediately after you succeed or fail on a {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB}, you can ignore the result and regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your level. You immediately rise to your feet and use Lightbringer's Glare (no action required). You don't expend a use of that trait, and the size of its {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} increases to 30 feet. Other creatures of your choice in that area regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Solar Mettle
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Radiant damage.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 4-5 feet tall)

entries.name: Aetheric Lock
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests} to avoid or end the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, or to avoid being moved from your space.

entries.name: Architect's Blessing
entries: When a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself makes a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} or {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}, you can enforce cosmic order and cause the roll to have neither Advantage nor Disadvantage. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Tireless Core
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws made to avoid or end the {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} condition.

Additionally, when you gain {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}, you can reduce the number of levels gained by 1. Once you use this trait to reduce an {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Unyielding Density
entries: When you take damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reduce the damage by {@dice 1d12} plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. The first time you deal damage before the end of your next turn, one target takes extra Force damage equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

You can take this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 5-6 feet tall)

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet.

entries.name: Gorgon's Gaze
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can fix your gaze on a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. The target is subjected to the effects of one of the options below, determined when you select this species. The save DC is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} and your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier (choose the ability when you select this species).

You can use this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

items.name: Beam
entries: Eroding rays fire from your eyes. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 1d6} Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. This damage increases by {@dice 1d6} when you reach character levels 5 ({@dice 2d6}), 11 ({@dice 3d6}), and 17 ({@dice 4d6}).

items.name: Hypnotic
entries: Your eyes become captivating pools. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Charmed condition for 1 minute. If you or your allies damage the target or force it to make a saving throw, the condition ends on it, and it is immune to your gaze for 24 hours.

items.name: Petrifying
entries: Your eyes flare with dull color. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have the Restrained condition for 1 minute from partially turning to stone. A creature with {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the Petrified condition succeeds automatically. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entries.name: Keen Senses
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Insight|XPHB}, {@skill Perception|XPHB}, or {@skill Survival|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Toxin Resistant
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Poison damage, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the Poisoned condition.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 5-7 feet tall) or Small (about 3-4 feet tall), chosen when you select this species

entries.name: Acid Resistance
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Acid damage.

entries.name: Amorphous
entries: You have a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and you can move through a space as narrow as 1 foot without expending extra movement to do so.

entries.name: Elastic Lunge
entries: You add 10 feet to your maximum {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB} distance and 5 feet to your maximum {@variantrule High Jump|XPHB} distance. You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to damage you take from falling.

entries.name: Malleable Reach
entries: Your reach with Melee weapons, {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}, and your hands to manipulate objects increases by 5 feet. Additionally, when you hit with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} and deal damage with it, you can deal Acid damage equal to {@dice 1d6} plus your Strength modifier, instead of the normal damage of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

entries.name: Reconstitution
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you expend and roll one {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die}. You regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the number rolled plus your Constitution modifier.

Alternatively, when you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} but not killed outright, you can immediately use this trait (no action required), and you can't activate it this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Tremorsense
entries: You have {@sense Tremorsense|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 5-7 feet tall) or Small (about 3-4 feet tall), determined by your Saurian Lineage trait

entries.name: Ancestral Memory
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Arcana|XPHB}, {@skill History|XPHB}, or {@skill Religion|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Harmonious Conduit
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you touch a creature and roll a number of d6s equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. The target regains a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the total rolled. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Natural Weapon
entries: You have a natural weapon you can use to make {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}. It deals {@damage 1d6} damage of a type appropriate to the weapon instead of the normal damage; Bludgeoning for a slam (club tail or headbutt), Piercing for a bite or gore, or Slashing for a claw. Your Saurian Lineage trait determines your natural weapon.

entries.name: Saurian Lineage
entries: You are part of a lineage that determines some of your physical characteristics and traits. Choose a lineage from the Saurian Lineages table and gain the benefits of that lineage.

entries.caption: Saurian Lineages
rows: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage and a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and you can hold your breath for a number of hours equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1).
You have proficiency in the {@skill Stealth|XPHB} skill, and while you are {@variantrule Lightly Obscured|XPHB}, you can hide while in an enemy's line of sight.
You have a +1 bonus to AC; your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by 1, and by 1 again each time you gain a level.

You can make a bite attack as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. When you hit a creature with your bite, you can deal extra damage equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Constitution modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. Once you deal extra damage in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
You have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. You can't fly while wearing Medium or Heavy armor.

items.name: Saurian Lineage (Deepfin)
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage and a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and you can hold your breath for a number of hours equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1).

items.name: Bite
entries: You have a natural weapon you can use to make {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}. It deals {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage.

items.name: Slam
entries: You have a natural weapon you can use to make {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}. It deals {@damage 1d6} Bludgeoning damage.

items.name: Saurian Lineage (Quickclaw)
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Stealth|XPHB} skill, and while you are {@variantrule Lightly Obscured|XPHB}, you can hide while in an enemy's line of sight.

items.name: Bite
entries: You have a natural weapon you can use to make {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}. It deals {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage.

items.name: Claw
entries: You have a natural weapon you can use to make {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}. It deals {@damage 1d6} Slashing damage.

items.name: Saurian Lineage (Rockhide)
entries: You have a +1 bonus to AC; your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by 1, and by 1 again each time you gain a level.

items.name: Slam
entries: You have a natural weapon you can use to make {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}. It deals {@damage 1d6} Bludgeoning damage.

items.name: Gore
entries: You have a natural weapon you can use to make {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}. It deals {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage.

items.name: Saurian Lineage (Sharptooth)
entries: You can make a bite attack as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. When you hit a creature with your bite, you can deal extra damage equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Constitution modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. Once you deal extra damage in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

items.name: Bite
entries: You have a natural weapon you can use to make {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}. It deals {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage.

items.name: Saurian Lineage (Skywing)
entries: You have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. You can't fly while wearing Medium or Heavy armor.

items.name: Claw
entries: You have a natural weapon you can use to make {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}. It deals {@damage 1d6} Slashing damage.

raceFluff.name: Glowblin
entries: Glowblins (GLOH-blin) are small folk who feel big and intense emotions. This wellspring of emotional energy colors their physical appearance and radiates out from them with neon light. Many glowblins tap into this inner magic to entertain and influence those around them, shaping light and sound to evoke different feelings in others.

Glowblins have colorful skin, hair, and large, luminous eyes. The colors change to match their affinity for different light and emotions and take on a wide spectrum of hues and shades within those colors. They also have long, expressive ears, sharp teeth, and pointed noses.

raceFluff.name: Raizo
entries: Raizo (RYE-zoh) hail from a storm-swept world, bound tightly to the tempestuous nature of the world soul that gives the planet—and its ferocious storms—life. All raizo carry the strength and fury of the storm within them and can unleash that power to overcome staggering odds.

Raizo are tall, solidly built, and often heavily muscled. A pair of horns grow from the top of their foreheads. Their skin runs in shades of red, blue, green, gray, or yellow. Many raizo sport tattoos that reflect particularly important aspects of their nature or personality.

raceFluff.name: Saurian
entries: The dinosaur-like saurians (SOR-ee-in) are one of the oldest species in the Stardust Rhapsody without question, though their assertion that they were the first sapient beings is impossible to verify. Saurians are touched by the Harmony and can channel lifegiving energies through that spark. Their home world grows with abundant harmonyte crystals that help them focus and hone that energy.

Across several lineages, saurians comprise a wide range of body types and statures. Each one is reminiscent of broader types of dinosaurs, showing traits of water-dwellers, fliers, great and small carnivores, and hardy herbivores.

raceFluff.name: Solari
entries: Eons ago, the solari (soh-LAH-ree) were consumed in an exploding sun but were reborn by the grace of Alara the Lightbringer. It is through this kinship with the galaxy's suns and the solar phoenixes that hatch from within them that the solari are bestowed with light and resplendence.

Solari tend to be graceful with especially long, pointed ears and floating hair that spans a gradient of vibrant hues. More muted but covering a broad gamut of warm tones, their skin tone encompasses pale pinks and oranges of sunset, hearty chestnut, and soft terracotta.

raceFluff.name: Aetheron
entries: Aetheron (EE-ther-awn) are a resilient people. While they generally reside in massive space stations, they are able to resist the effects of exposure in space and are possessed of the supernatural ability to manipulate aspects of aether within themselves.

Prevalent among many Aetheron is the belief they were crafted by Khorabos the Architect from a proto form of aether. If not for the god's handiwork, for what reason would the cosmic order so strongly assert itself in their presence?

Aetheron are relatively short, often stoutly built, and are heavier than their size would suggest. Their skin tones are usually muted with casts of dull gray, blue, or purple, but they often have vibrant streaks of aether hues in their hair.

raceFluff.name: Gorgonite
entries: The serpentine features and eyes that gleam with supernatural power are telltale signs of the gorgonite's (GOR-guh-nite) origin. Their home world teems with venomous fauna and toxic flora of all kinds and sizes, and the gorgonites' connection to its world soul protects them from the worst toxins and prepares them to face numerous dangers.
Gorgonites have a skin of soft scales that range from vivid pigments to earthy shades. Instead of hair atop their heads, tendrils reminiscent of snake tails drape like curtains or curl into knots.

raceFluff.name: Oozoid
entries: Oozoids (OO-zoid) are a pliable species of living slime that congealed on the fluid world of Tibbulus from the remnants of the first primordial ooze. Despite their malleability, they have a brain, eyes, and nervous system that form them into a bipedal shape within a translucent membrane.

Bright and highly saturated colors of slime typically comprise the wide variety of shapes and sizes of oozoids. Some favor a singular color, while others are multi-hued with strong gradients. Many choose to morph their heads to evoke hair-like shapes or even form teeth out of hardened resin.
